top_comments_sample: Incredible achievement but I'll be honest — if you showed me this photo without context I would have no idea it was the far side. Just looks like the Moon. Also didn't realize we could capture an image like this in what I assumed was total darkness.
They have live tracker you can follow https://www.nasa.gov/missions/artemis-ii/arow/

I'm going to be VERY disappointed if there's no Pink Floyd music or commentary from the Artemis mission. Particularly now. Life's short, and one can't be serious all the time... Wallis and Gromit would be a partial substitute, but the boomers are still around.

I like how most people's reactions at this point are "yeah, whatever", as if it's every day that humans observe the far side of the moon with a naked eye through a window :). We do know what it looks like and we have photos from the surface, yes, but seeing the reaction from real people who're actually there does hit different, at least for me
There’s too many problems here on earth for me to get excited about a trip to the moon
I am curious. If it is on the far side, where does the light come from for the photos? Other stars?

Am I losing it? They can’t be seeing the far side of the moon right now, because they haven’t adjusted course to go round the far side of the moon yet… So does this suggest the BBC is wrong and it’s the side of the moon we’re used to seeing, but just it’s “dark”? But then the astronauts are saying it’s weird seeing the moon in a whole new light (excuse the paraphrasing pun). I don’t understand.
It makes me tear up seeing the absolute 'best of us' as humanity striving and exploring in the midst of so much wretched evil and awfulness.

community_verdict.summary: 评论者对Artemis II宇航员首次目睹月球远侧表达了惊奇和成就感，尽管有些人指出照片看起来平凡或对远侧与暗侧的区别进行澄清。部分评论显示出兴奋和情感共鸣，如感动于人类探索精神，但也存在困惑关于照明和航行路径，以及对地球问题优先的冷漠态度。整体讨论反映了成就认可与现实主义质疑的混合。 用户反馈既包含认可，也提出了执行层面的质疑。 对 PM 来说，关键是验证需求强度与可持续增长。
